event_info: short of a length around off; shaping away from the batter; Jacob Bethell has a poke at it; gets beaten due to the movement
on the shorter side outside off; Jacob Bethell rides the bounce; steering it away for the first run of the match
that's a lovely delivery by Mohammed Siraj; outside off and shaping away from the batter; Virat Kohli has a poke and gets beaten

almost chopped on. Back of a length delivery around off; shapes in slightly as Virat Kohli plays it with soft hands. The ball almost ricochets onto the stumps. They take a single by the time the keeper moves forward to gather it
drifting on the pads; it's a length delivery that Jacob Bethell clips towards mid-wicket

first boundary of the match! Short and wide outside off; Jacob Bethell throws the kitchen sink at it; slashing it away to the deep backward point boundary. No chance of stopping that as the fielder at third man was pretty fine. A decent over by Mohammed Siraj; barring this boundary ball
short and punished! Virat Kohli into the act now. This was on the shorter side; Virat Kohli pulls it over mid-wicket for a boundary. Kagiso Rabada being greeted with a boundary
back-to-back boundaries! Back of a length delivery around off; Virat Kohli plays it on the rise; lofting it over wide mid-off. Clears the infield comfortably and that's four more
hat-trick of boundaries! Virat Kohli on the charge. Short of a length delivery outside off; Virat Kohli plays it late; carving this away to the deep point fence

4;4;4;4. Now towards the left side of the fielder at square third man. Back of a length delivery outside off; Virat Kohli carves it away to the fence once again. The fielder in the deep couldn't do much about it

make that five in a row. All class. This was pitched up by the bowler outside off and Virat Kohli creamed it through covers for the 5th consecutive boundary. Kagiso Rabada has been among the wickets this season; but Kohli has absolutely torn him apart in his 1st over itself
pitched up by the bowler as Virat Kohli drives it right down the pitch; as Kagiso Rabada gets his shoe to it; deflecting it away towards mid-on
back of a length around middle and leg; keeps a touch low; Virat Kohli looks to nudge it away; gets an inside edge onto the pads. That inside edge has saved him; else it looked an lbw dismissal
more runs for Virat Kohli! He advances down the wicket and tonks the length delivery over long-on for the first maximum of the evening. RCB; RCB chants all across the Narendra Modi Stadium
1 run; fired on leg; it's a length delivery; Virat Kohli looks to whip it away; gets hit on the pads though. A leg bye taken

ball_event: out Caught by Rashid Khan!! Mohammed Siraj draws first blood. Runs were coming thick and fast
event_info: but Mohammed Siraj takes a wicket. That was a pretty soft dismissal though. Was a length delivery on middle and leg; Jacob Bethell flicks it straight to the man at mid-wicket. Rashid Khan takes a safe catch; and the left-handed opener is going without making an impact. Think Bethell was dismissed by Siraj in the reverse fixture at the Chinnaswamy too. Jacob Bethell c Rashid Khan b Mohammed Siraj 5(5) [4s-1]
short and wide outside off; some extra bounce; Devdutt Padikkal looks to cut it on the rise; gets beaten due to the bounce though. Good amount of carry
fraction short around off; Devdutt Padikkal plays it on the up; knocking it towards mid-off
not a great line as Kagiso Rabada sprays it down the leg-side. A gentle glance would have fetched Virat Kohli a boundary; but he fails to make contact
good length delivery that skids along the deck; Virat Kohli looks to defend; gets an inside edge onto the pads

ball_event: out Caught by Rashid Khan!! Kagiso Rabada has the last laugh! After conceding five boundaries in his 1st over
event_info: Kagiso Rabada roars back in style. He looks animated as soon as Rashid Khan takes this catch. That's the quality he has. Didn't get bogged down despite VK taking him on. This was a short and quick delivery around middle and leg; Virat Kohli gets hurried up due to the pace and bounce as he advances down the wicket. Loses his shape while playing this across the line and the top edge has been taken by the fielder at mid-wicket. Gives the batsman as a stare on his way back to the pavilion. Virat Kohli c Rashid Khan b Kagiso Rabada 28(13) [4s-5 6s-1]
defended watchfully by Rajat Patidar
Rajat Patidar is off the mark in style. This was pitched up by the bowler and Rajat Patidar drives it straight past KG. Played it as straight as an arrow and gets going with a boundary
drags back his length slightly; Rajat Patidar defends it from within the crease
defends it on the rise; knocking it back to the pacer. A much better over by Rabada as compared to the previous one
on a length around off; Devdutt Padikkal drives it to the fielder at cover

smoked over deep mid-wicket! Fraction short; Devdutt Padikkal picks the length early and hoicks it over the deep mid-wicket fence for a 73m maximum. Mohammed Siraj immediately changes his direction after delivering that; starts walking backing to his run-up mark
slashed away! Short in length outside off; Devdutt Padikkal makes room and throws the kitchen sink at it; gets a top edge that flies down to the third man fence
fraction short outside off; Devdutt Padikkal steers it towards cover-point
on the shorter side around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal advances down the wicket and clips this towards mid-wicket

50 up for RCB! This was short again; Devdutt Padikkal gets hurried up due to the pace; goes for the pull and manages to clear Rashid Khan at mid-wicket. He chases it all the way to the fence and puts in a dive; but can't stop the boundary
146 clicks; short and quick outside off; Rajat Patidar looks to cut this on the rise; gets beaten on the outside edge though

Kagiso Rabada almost with his 2nd wicket. That's a ripper of a delivery. Short and quick around middle; Rajat Patidar had no clue about this one. Ends up getting a top edge towards third man; and manages to beat the fielder who was converging across from third man. Some other day; this would've been a catch
steered towards short third man as he opens the bat face to play it
defended by the batsman from within the crease
back of a length delivery around off; Rajat Patidar plays it late; steering it to backward point
at the batter's hips as Devdutt Padikkal tucks it through square leg for a single
the approach is going to be the same. That shot by Devdutt Padikkal is an evidence. He makes room by moving away and carves this short and wide ball over backward point. No chance of stopping that

more runs! This was a short and wide delivery outside off; Devdutt Padikkal throws his hands at it; punching it powerfully through the off-side. Once again the fielder couldn't do much about it; he had to cover quite some ground
better stop by third man. Was fraction short and slow outside off; Devdutt Padikkal steers it soft hands and has to settle for one
short of a length outside off; Rajat Patidar glides it down to third man
down the wicket Devdutt Padikkal advances; nudging this through backward square leg
nudged to long-on by Rajat Patidar for a single

Arshad Khan being greeted with a maximum. He goes short and Rajat Patidar picks the length early; transfers his weight onto the back foot and pulls it over deep mid-wicket for a flat six does the RCB skipper
tad short on middle and leg; Rajat Patidar nudges it towards mid-wicket for a couple of runs
short and quick on leg; Rajat Patidar sways away from its line

ball_event: out Caught by Jason Holder!! That's a fantastic catch by Holder. One of the best of IPL 2026
event_info: probably. Was a short ball by Arshad Khan and Rajat Patidar went for a pull; gets a top edge towards deep backward square leg. Holder stationed there charges to his right and takes a fine low catch. Rabada too was charging across to take it; but Holder managed to ignore that. RCB players are having an argument with the umpires. They think Holder wasn't in control of the catch and had touched the turf. But the umpires reckon otherwise. Andy Flower still having a chat with the fourth umpire. Rajat Patidar c Jason Holder b Arshad Khan 19(15) [4s-2 6s-1]
back of a length around off; Jitesh Sharma defends it from within the crease
steered towards backward point by Jitesh Sharma for a single

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! Jitesh Sharma's poor run in IPL 2026 continues. Holder into the act again. Took a fabulous catch in the previous over
event_info: takes a wicket in this over. And he's absolutely pumped up. This was pitched up by him outside off and the batter was enticed into a drive. A thick outside edge is induced and Buttler behind the wickets completes a well-judged catch moving to his right. They're scoring at a brisk rate; but are losing wickets simultaneously. Jitesh Sharma c Jos Buttler b Jason Holder 1(3)
on the shorter side outside off; slapped into the off-side by Tim David
at the hips; nudged towards short fine leg
Jason Holder goes short and quick from over the wicket; Devdutt Padikkal slaps it over the bowler; a single taken at long-on

brutal! Shot of an absolutely powerful batsman. This was on the shorter side and Tim David muscles it over deep mid-wicket for a flat six. Looks like he was batting somewhere in the nets before coming in
tad short outside off; Tim David slaps it through the off-side for a single
fraction short outside off; Tim David plays a late cut; straight to Siraj on the bounce at short third
Rashid Khan goes full as Tim David drills it along the carpet to long-off
on the shorter side; Devdutt Padikkal rocks back and drags it through mid-wicket for a single

ball_event: out Caught by Jason Holder!! Holder on a roll. He's turning this game on his head
event_info: his catching has been extraordinary today. That catch of Patidar earlier; and now this sharp take at mid-wicket. Rashid Khan with a wicket in his first over. This was whipped hard by the batsman and Holder took a sharp grab towards his right-side. Hardly had any time to reach; so that's quite impressive. RCB have lost half their side in Ahmedabad. Momentum shifting like the pendulum of a clock. Tim David c Jason Holder b Rashid Khan 9(6) [6s-1]
full and straight on leg; Krunal Pandya looks to nudge it away; gets hit on the pads though
clipped with soft hands towards the on-side by Krunal Pandya. A successful over by Rashid Khan. Only a couple of runs and a wicket in it
tad short on middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal clips it towards long-on
tad short outside off; Krunal Pandya plays it late; carving it away to the square third man fence. Even he gets going with a boundary just like most of the RCB batters today

ball_event: out Caught by Jason Holder!! Holder is everywhere. A length delivery around middle and leg
event_info: Krunal Pandya flicks it straight to mid-wicket where the big man takes it again. Arshad Khan has his arms wide open as he takes his second wicket. RCB will likely need an impact sub in the form of Venkatesh Iyer today. They've faced this issue earlier in the tournament; where Venky Iyer was required. That might be the case even in this match. Meanwhile; GT on top as they are taking wickets at regular intervals. Krunal Pandya c Jason Holder b Arshad Khan 4(4) [4s-1]
pitched up by the bowler outside off; Romario Shepherd square drives it towards point
drifting on the pads; clipped to mid-on by Romario Shepherd
short ball on leg; Romario Shepherd pulls it through backward square leg
tossed up delivery by Rashid Khan as Romario Shepherd drives this to long-on for a single
on the fuller side around leg; Devdutt Padikkal looks to flick it away; gets his on the pads though
driven along the carpet to long-on for one
fraction short outside off; Romario Shepherd has a poke at it; gets beaten on the outside edge
100 up for RCB. Romario Shepherd bludgeons this towards long-on for one
full and straight outside off; Devdutt Padikkal push-drives it towards cover. Another wily over by Rashid Khan - he has gone for 5 runs in 2 overs; add a wicket to that
1 run; length delivery around middle and leg; Romario Shepherd looks to nudge it away; gets hit on the pads though; and they've taken a leg bye

a good effort by Gill in the deep; but I think he was in contact with the cushions. Devdutt Padikkal had punched it through wide mid-off and Gill tried to chase it and flick it away; but he was in contact with the boundary at the same time

that has gone all the way. Devdutt Padikkal took his eyes off the ball while playing this short ball. Somehow manages to connect it and clears the boundary at fine leg. It was due to Rabada's raw pace that the ball flew away
steered towards short third man for a single
good length delivery outside off; Romario Shepherd opens the bat face; steering it towards backward point
fraction short outside off; Romario Shepherd opens the bat face; steering this towards point for one
sharp leg-break outside off; Romario Shepherd looks to defend; gets beaten on the outside edge

smoked into the sightscreen! Rashid Khan goes full and Romario Shepherd has launched it for a handsome six. He's got ability; just that he needs to be wary against Rashid. Can always shift gears against rest of the GT bowlers
flatter and trifle short; Shepherd pushes it back to the bowler
another biggie; he's picking Khan well; tossed up and on middle; Shepherd picks it and smashes it across the line over deep mid-wicket; bit too full from Rashid
tossed up and touch slower; Shepherd was beaten by the pace and drags it down to long-on

ball_event: out Bowled!! There's the big strike
event_info: Dev was deprived of strike as he was losing partners at the other end and now he departs; this one was pushed through quicker on middle; Dev rocks back to defend; but gets into a tangle; as he edges it onto the pads and then the ball ricochets onto the stumps. Devdutt Padikkal b Rashid Khan 40(24) [4s-5 6s-2]

ball_event: out Caught by Sai Sudharsan!! It's Holder again
event_info: you can't keep him out of action tonight; back of a length and outside off; Shepherd was shaping in to pull; but didn't get it off the middle of the bat and hits it straight into the hands of Sai in the deep; RCB sink further. Romario Shepherd c Sai Sudharsan b Jason Holder 17(15) [6s-2]

he's off the mark; full and outside off; Kumar flicks it along the ground to deep mid-wicket; Siraj puts in the dive and pulls it back; but it was going towards the ropes and another fielder puts in the dive and saves a run
full and outside off; Iyer pushes it down to mid-off
back of a length and outside off; Kumar gets right behind and defends it
back of a length and outside off; Kumar taps it to cover point
short and outside off; Iyer hangs back and guides it down to third man
tossed up delivery on the pads; Iyer misses out on the flick shot
this one hurried onto him as he was struck on the back foot; he's hit high on the pads; stifle appeal for an LBW
turning into the pads; Iyer is not picking Rashid here; flicks it to square leg
tossed up and on middle; this one turned away; Kumar defends it to the off side
floated up and bit slower; Kumar gets forward and defends it
well bowled from Rashid; quicker and outside off; Kumar stays back and pushes it to covers
well bowled; short on the stumps; Iyer moves across to the off side and attempts the paddle shot; but missed it completely
1 run; Iyer is not able to pick tonight; once again moves across the off side and misses out on the paddle scoop
back of a length and outside off; Kumar guides it down to square third man
short and on the stumps; it was bit slower; Iyer pulls it straight to the fielder at short fine leg
banged in short; Iyer comes down the track and drags it down to long-on
back of a length and just outside off; cramping him for any room; Bhuvneshwar pushes it to point; straight to the fielder
banged in short; way too high over the batsmen; the square leg umpire signals it as a wide
he's hit on the elbow; he's in some pain as the physio strides out; this one was short and outside off; Iyer shapes into pull; but was early into the shot and is hit flush on the elbow
full and sliding into the pads; Iyer flicks it away to deep square leg
beaten all ends up; good length and slanting across the right hander; Bhuvneshwar pokes at it and misses

what a shot from Bhuvi; a boundary after 23 balls; short and outside off; Kumar arches back; gets on top fo the ball nicely and punches it off the back foot to sweeper cover; excellent timing on that one
good length delivery on middle and leg; Kumar plays it to covers
angling across the right hander; Kumar taps it to point
angling in from outside off; Kumar stays back and defends it
Bhuvi is on fire; good length delivery outside off; hint of some room; Kumar makes room and hits it on the up to sweeper cover
back of a length outside off; Kumar goes back and cuts it to cover
short and wide outside off; Iyer gets on top of the bounce and cuts it in the air to third man; he didn't time it well
well struck; pitched up and in the slot; Iyer clears his front leg and drills it down the ground to long-off; excellent timing on that
banged in short on the stumps; Iyer stands tall and taps it to backward point

ball_event: out Caught by Sai Sudharsan!! Sharp catch in the deep by Sai
event_info: full and angling into the stumps; Iyer swings it across the line towards midwicket; the bat turned in his hand; but he still goes through the shot and hits it in the air towards; Sai dives to his left; initially didn't hold onto it; but takes it in the end. Venkatesh Iyer c Sai Sudharsan b Arshad Khan 12(15) [4s-1]

ball_event: out Josh Hazlewood Run Out!! A terrible mix-up in the end
event_info: first time RCB have been bowled out this season; back of a length delivery outside off; Hazlewood pushes at it and misses; he tries to steal a bye; but; Butler was quick to hit the stumps direct at the striker's end; Hazlewood had just made it to the crease and then tries to steal a run; but Gill was quick to respond at the non-strikers end and hits the stumps with Hazlewood well short of the crease. Josh Hazlewood run out (Shubman Gill) 0(1)
short of length and tight on off; drop and run in front of point and Sai Sudharsan is away

superb inswinger and the thick inside-edge just eludes the dive of Jitesh Sharma to his left. That's a fine delivery; nips back in and Shubman Gill is late on the back foot block; the thick inside-edge misses the dive and the outstretched left hand. Kohli has his hands on his head

what a shot. Shubman Gill looks a million dollars when he plays shots like that. And look at him hold the pose. He loved that. Full outside off; Gill gets forward and lofts over extra-cover; all timing and this flies over the deep extra-cover fence. That's the shot of the game so far

fantastic comeback. This is a beautiful delivery. Squares Shubman Gill up. Bhuvneshwar Kumar so unlucky not to get the outside-edge. It pitches around off; short of length and snakes away so late; Gill is beaten for pace as well; is lucky not to edge that

short of length and angling into the right-hander on leg-stump; Shubman Gill closes the face of the bat too early; the thick leading-edge rolls through the point area; went down even though it was in the air for a while
full outside off; Sai Sudharsan takes a step forward; it's angling away from the batter; he watchfully lets it go. Good delivery and Sudharsan respected that

Shubman Gill on the attack. Against Josh Hazlewood. He comes down the track and cuts the short of length delivery on middle; up and over backward point. Good shot that and a good way to put pressure on the bowler
more good batting. Again he steps out; makes room outside the leg-stump; this is there; he lofts and it's up and over mid-off and extra-cover. Immediately Josh Hazlewood under pressure
a wide slip waits as Josh Hazlewood fires the yorker length delivery outside off; Shubman Gill gets forward to drive and digs it out back to the bowler; who stops it to his left

mighty loft. Full and in the slot just outside off; Shubman Gill gets forward and lofts through the line; again Gill looks at the flight of the ball; that came off the sweet part of the bat and it flies over long-on. Mid-on was inside

Shubman Gill on fire. He backs away outside the leg-stump; it's short of length and following him there; but it's just a punch square of the wicket and he splits backward point and cover-point. Even Josh Hazlewood with a resigned wry smile on his face.

Shubman Gill with the walk of a prince and the swag of a king. He has taken 24 off the Josh Hazlewood over. This is full and down leg; poor delivery and Gill gets forward to flick effortlessly over deep backward square-leg. After the ball flew off the bat; Gill walked with a swag as he had a look at the RCB fielders. What a start for GT

full outside off; angling away from the left-hander; Sai Sudharsan gets forward to drive; the thick outside-edge flies well wide of the wide slip and runs away past short third man too; to his left. Runs leaking here
short of length and on middle; Sai Sudharsan comes down the track to punch to the right of mid-wicket; they take a quick single

short of length and on leg-stump; moves away; Shubman Gill comes down the track and makes room to cut; misses; the ball flies over the bat and a flat line on UltraEdge. Bhuvneshwar Kumar immediately went up for a caught behind appeal. Jitesh Sharma even tried for the run-out at Gill's end; misses. Patidar takes the review and loses it
short of length on off; Shubman Gill goes on the back foot to pull; that hurried him; the thick inside-edge hits the thigh pad and goes to his left
short of length on off; drop and run to the vacant backward point area. Shubman Gill was solidly behind the line there

ball_event: out Caught by Jitesh Sharma!! Fine delivery and RCB have the wicket. Full on off
event_info: hint of shape away; tight delivery and Sai Sudharsan wanted to drive on the up; the thick outside-edge is found; good catch as Jitesh Sharma dives to his left and takes it with both hands. Bit loose from Sai there but that was a good delivery. Kohli is pumped and shows his emotions. Sai Sudharsan c Jitesh Sharma b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 6(5) [4s-1]

good delivery. Shubman Gill comes down the track and makes room outside the leg-stump; Josh Hazlewood probably spotted that; fires the yorker length delivery inside the tramline on the off-side; Gill can't reach it; so lets it go

Kohli with a needless show of aggression and he gives away four more. A tight short of length delivery on off; Shubman Gill on the defence; gets a thick inside-edge on to the pad; the ball rolls in front of cover; Kohli fires it way wide of mid-on and no chance for Venky Iyer there; he put in the dive to his right but the ball had pace and was well wide of his reach. Kohli immediately thumps his thigh with his hand in frustration. Meanwhile Gill and Jos Buttler have a smile on their faces as they get together. There was no chance of a run-out there and it was not needed
tight length delivery outside off; Jos Buttler gets forward to push it with an open-face in front of backward point for one
length delivery outside off; Shubman Gill gets forward and opens the face of the bat to guide it to deep backward point. Just one

dropped by Jitesh Sharma. He went one-handed to his right and fluffs it. It's a good short delivery outside off; Jos Buttler often plays this shot; he shuffles across and looks to scoop; it gets big on him; off the glove and even though it moved away at the end; Jitesh is in no position to take that; goes with an outstretched right hand and can't hang on. Should he have left that to the wide slip?

that's the shot he was trying last ball. This is on middle-stump; short of length and Jos Buttler shuffles across and nails the scoop. Middles it and it flies over Jitesh Sharma and into the fine-leg stands

direct-hit and Shubman Gill was gone. Tight short of length delivery on off; Shubman Gill gets forward to defend; Kohli at cover cuts off the angle; but was off-balance and diving when he threw that; well wide of the stumps and even a full-length dive - which Gill put - wouldn't have saved him. Another chance and it goes
tight length delivery outside off; Jos Buttler gets forward and pushes it to backward point; slightly open-face
short of length and no room on off; Jos Buttler gets forward to defend it back to Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Defended off the full-face
Jos Buttler tries to manufacture something. He comes down the track and looks to drive the full delivery outside off; on the rise; hint of away movement and he misses. Close to the outside-edge
Jos Buttler shuffles across outside off; this is full outside off; he pushes it in front of mid-off and they take a single

ball_event: out Caught by Virat Kohli!! Look at Kohli. He takes it and flings the ball into the turf and then looks at Shubman Gill as he mouths a "come on". He is pumped. Meanwhile Gill just puts his head back in disappointment. He nailed this shot but straight to the fielder at cover. It's a length delivery and Gill comes down the track and makes room outside the leg-stump
event_info: drilled that; but Kohli reverse-cups near his neck. That was travelling and a good catch. Kohli has been trying to get inside the skin of these GT batters and it has worked. Shubman Gill c Virat Kohli b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 43(18) [4s-4 6s-3]
short of length outside off; Jos Buttler goes on the back foot and cuts hard and straight to deep backward point
short of length outside off; angling away from the left-hander; Washington Sundar goes on the back foot and opens the face of the bat to drive; the ball finds the square third man
short of length outside off; Jos Buttler goes for a drive on the rise and misses. So close to the outside-edge
wide of the crease and short of length; nips back in a tad; Jos Buttler gets forward to defend to the right of Josh Hazlewood. More off the thick inner half of the bat
full outside off; that's in the slot and Jos Buttler gets forward to loft over cover and mid-off. Typical Buttler shot. In the air but in the gap too

this is an even better shot. Full and angling into the right-hander on off; Jos Buttler takes it on and gets forward to loft through the line; clean strike and mid-on is inside. Flies all the way over the long-on fence. Ends the powerplay well
full outside off; Washington Sundar gets forward to drive to sweeper cover. Krunal with a slight fumble but no damage done
full down leg; Jos Buttler sweeps and misses; called a wide
tossed up and in the slot on middle; Jos Buttler is seeing them well; gets forward and lofts straight down the ground. Towards the sightscreen. So easy

more flight and six more. Can't bowl there to Jos Buttler. Not in this form. A hat-trick of sixes for Buttler. It's tossed up full outside off; Buttler gets forward and slams the loft over long-off. The fielder was a spectator
Suyash Sharma bowls this quicker and on a back of a length on middle; no turn; Jos Buttler goes on the back foot and nudges it down to long-on
the googly and it's tossed up full outside off; Washington Sundar gets forward to drive to sweeper cover
flat good length on off; Jos Buttler goes on the back foot and punches to sweeper cover

fine batting. Jos Buttler shuffles across and this is a length delivery outside off; he also takes a step or two out of the crease; flicks and beats mid-wicket to his right. No-one back and it runs away
short of length outside off; Jos Buttler again shuffles across and gets forward to whip this between fine-leg and deep backward square-leg; easy two

ball_event: out Bowled!! That's so canny from Bhuvneshwar Kumar. He has completely done Jos Buttler here. Once again he shuffles across and this time it's the very full delivery on leg-stump
event_info: Buttler is beaten for pace and also he couldn't get the bat down as the pad was in the way. He got into a tangle and the ball knocks out leg-stump. Immediately Bhuvneshwar goes to Kohli to celebrate. Looked like a plan there and it has worked. Massive wicket that as Buttler was the man who was looking ultra-aggressive. Jos Buttler b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 39(19) [4s-2 6s-4]

short of length outside off; Shahrukh Khan slashes and beats the dive of backward point to his left. But square third man is there; he runs across and cuts it off to his right. Just one. Hazlewood the man
tight good length delivery on off; slight angle away; Washington Sundar gets forward to push to cover
short of length on off and middle; Washington Sundar goes on the back foot and closes the face of the bat to tuck to fine-leg. End of Bhuvneshwar Kumar
short of length outside off; angling away from the left-hander; Washington Sundar goes on the back foot and cuts straight to the fielder at backward point
short of length on off; Washington Sundar goes on the back foot and forces this down to long-on

wait; wait was the call but backward point on the dive to his left can't stop that cleanly and then they take one. It's a tight short of length delivery on off; Shahrukh Khan goes on the back foot and defends
short of length outside off; Washington Sundar goes on the back foot and high-elbows the punch to mid-off; Shahrukh Khan is sent back in time. The fielder closed in quickly there
full just outside off; Washington Sundar gets forward to drive and the bat-face closes; he finds Romario Shepherd to his left

Venky Iyer at deep backward square-leg has made a meal of that. He should've been right back on the fence. Instead he is a few paces in and misjudges this. Tries to go with two hands; then goes with an outstretched left hand; on the leap. He got the leap all wrong and the chance goes. It's a short of length delivery on middle; Washington Sundar lifts one leg and helps the pull; the shot he plays so well; had Iyer been right back; he could've taken that easily. Instead the ball flicks the fingertips and goes all the way. Patidar and the other RCB players are disappointed
full outside off; loose drive from Shahrukh Khan as he looks to get it past cover; misses; close to the outside-edge
short of length and tight on off; neat back foot block to backward point
short of length on middle; Shahrukh Khan shuffles across and clips it down to deep backward square-leg
full down leg; Washington Sundar gets forward to flick to deep backward square-leg and Krunal keeps it to one

that's a good shot. Full on middle; Shahrukh Khan comes down the track and lofts through the line; knows mid-off is inside and he has hit that well. Resigned wry smile from Josh Hazlewood. Calculated blow that

va; va [come; come] is the cry from Shahrukh Khan in Tamil to Washington Sundar. It's a rising short of length delivery on off; Shahrukh drops it to the right of Josh Hazlewood; Washington didn't want to go till the ball crossed the bowler. Hazlewood can't cut it off and in the end it's an easy single

ball_event: out Caught by Josh Hazlewood!! Fat top-edge and Hazlewood at fine-leg takes a good catch. It's a short of length delivery outside off
event_info: Shahrukh Khan goes on the back foot to pull; but the top-edge flies and Hazlewood sinks on one knee and dives forward to take it with two hands. Almost misjudged that but took it well. RCB keep themselves in it with wickets. Shahrukh Khan c Josh Hazlewood b Romario Shepherd 8(8) [4s-1]
short and too short on the stumps; Rahul Tewatia lets it go and takes the wide
short of length on middle; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and turns it to the vacant forward short-leg area

ball_event: out Lbw!! Even the normally "placid" Bhuvi roars once he sees the big screen. That's what this means to RCB. And Kohli is punching the air and roaring as usual. It's a length delivery and on off
event_info: Washington Sundar gets forward to drive and misses; hit on the back leg. No bat as UltraEdge shows and it's three reds. Romario Shepherd was almost pleading with KN Anantha Padmanabhan to give it who stayed calm. Kohli and Jitesh Sharma convinced Patidar to take the review and it has worked. That was plumb. Wondwer why the ump didn't give it. RCB know they have a good chance now even though GT have batting left still. Washington Sundar lbw b Romario Shepherd 12(12) [6s-1]

the battle between the Guyanese and the Barbadian and it's the latter who lands the first blow. It's a short delivery at the tall Jason Holder who shuffles across slightly and hooks; Hazlewood was there; but it sails over his head. That was well-hit
length delivery outside off; Jason Holder gets forward and opens the face of the bat to guide it to third man. At the end; the two West Indians have a chat and a big smile from Romario Shepherd
full and down leg; Rahul Tewatia glances as he gets forward and plays it fine. Runs away to the fine-leg fence. Kohli is not happy with that delivery. Curses under his breath
full on middle; Jason Holder gets forward to drive to long-on
a slip waits. It's tossed up full outside off; Rahul Tewatia gets forward to defend to the left of slip. Wants a quick single; but Jason Holder is not interested
missing off and Patidar knows it. Was the tossed up googly; full and it's spinning away from off; Rahul Tewatia sinks low to sweep and misses. He's rapped on the pads
short and down leg; Rahul Tewatia has it easy; he goes on the back foot and pulls flat to the deep square-leg fence. Suyash Sharma with one or two local expletives as he is not happy with himself

that's risky but Rahul Tewatia has got bat on that. Premeditated and the reverse-sweep works. Turns around and takes it from middle-stump; hits it wide of backward point; to his right; no dive and the ball runs away
Suyash Sharma round the wicket. It's flat and short outside off; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and cuts to deep point
full outside off; Rahul Tewatia gets forward to drive; the thick inside-edge runs away well wide of leg-stump; that could've gone so easily back on
good inswinging yorker on middle; Jason Holder gets forward to dig it out
low full-toss and angling into the right-hander on middle; Jason Holder gets forward and shows the full face of the bat to drive to long-on
short of length outside off; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and cuts to sweeper cover
short outside off; Jason Holder goes on the back foot and pulls; misses; flies over the bat
short outside off; Jason Holder goes on the back foot and pulls; was in the air but wide of the dive of mid-wicket. To his right. They take an easy two
flat back of a length on off; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and nudges it to the vacant mid-wicket area
tossed up googly on a good length on off; Jason Holder gets forward to defend to the right of Suyash Sharma
tossed up full on off; turns a bit; quicker; Jason Holder gets forward to push; the thick outside-edge rolls away to short third man; they take a quick single
Jitesh Sharma looks at his right thumb. It's a flat delivery down leg; Rahul Tewatia wants to sweep that away; misses and Jitesh can't glove it. Did Tewatia's bat hit Jitesh?
on a length outside off; drop and run to the point region

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal at deep mid-wicket!! Jason Holder holes out in trying for the big hit. It's a tossed up googly on middle
event_info: Holder uses his long reach to get forward and heave; but doesn't have the elevation and picks out Padikkal to perfection. He didn't have to move an inch. Jason Holder c Devdutt Padikkal b Suyash Sharma 12(10) [6s-1]
nicely tossed up googly; spins into off and middle; Rashid Khan gets forward to defend and the thick inside-edge hits the pad and stays near his feet

dug in short and wide signalled by Adrian Holdstock. RCB can't believe that and Patidar reviews. Rahul Tewatia lets it go and is it over the head? Yes it is. RCB will lose this. They are waiting to check the ball-tracking on this. Tewatia's head is 1.75m and the ball is at 1.79m. Good call from Holdstock from square-leg
good length and tight on off; Rahul Tewatia gets forward to defend to backward point. No run there
short of length just outside off; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and opens the face of the bat to guide it to third man
full on off; Rashid Khan gets forward to drive to mid-off. They take a quick single
Romario Shepherd round the wicket. It's a short of length and angling into the left-hander on off; gently nudged to the vacant square-leg off the back foot
tight length delivery just outside off; Rashid Khan goes on the back foot and high-elbows the punch back to Romario Shepherd
short on the stumps; Rashid Khan ducks and lets it go
tossed up and full outside off; the off-side field is up. Easy for Rahul Tewatia; he gets forward and lofts over extra-cover; runs away
flat good length and quicker outside off; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and cuts wide of deep point; David runs across and cuts it off to his right
flat back of a length on middle; still quick; Rahul Tewatia goes on the back foot and pulls to deep backward square-leg
tossed up full outside off; Rashid Khan gets low and sweeps; Shepherd at square-leg dives to his left and gets a hand on it; prevents the four. David runs across and cuts it off from deep mid-wicket

Rashid Khan ends this. It's a flat length delivery outside off; he comes down the track and even though he is not to the pitch of the ball; he flat-bats that and it's one bounce over the ropes at deep extra-cover. A big hug and smile from Kohli to Rashid
